summary: Iteration 26 backend-only verification of navigation merge + PageBuilder override + sidebar-configs. 19/19 pytest tests passed. All review_request items verified.
tests_performed: GET /api/navigation/public (no auth) -> 200 with items list (regression from iter25 where it returned 401 is FIXED)
Default Blog/Videos/News/About paths present; items sorted by sort_order
Created published+public PageBuilder page (show_in_nav=true) -> appears at /page/<slug> exactly once with is_pagebuilder:true
Created reserved-slug PageBuilder page (slug='videos') -> appears at /videos exactly once (no duplicate with default)
Nav CRUD: POST /api/navigation, PATCH /api/navigation/{id}, DELETE /api/navigation/{id} -> all 200
critical_code_review_comments: navigation.py /public merge logic: reserved-slug override check uses path-set dedupe (existing_paths). PageBuilder items are appended BEFORE defaults, so a PageBuilder page with reserved slug correctly suppresses the default. Verified via test.
pages.py GET /pages/custom/{slug}: unpublished + private pages correctly return 404 (not 403) to hide existence. Good security posture.
navigation.py /public currently returns up to 200 navigation_items + 200 custom_pages. Fine for now but consider pagination/caching at scale.
sidebar_configs.py: GET list returns plain list (not wrapped in {configs:[]}) while other list endpoints use wrapped objects -- minor inconsistency, frontend must handle.
seed_data_creation: Test pages/nav items/sidebar-configs created+deleted within each test; no lingering TEST_ data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Navigation /public regression (was 401 in iter25) is FIXED in iter26. PageBuilder auto-sync to public nav is working correctly with dedupe and reserved-slug mapping. Admin token lives in 'access_token' field of login response. When creating custom_pages for tests, use slug pattern TEST_{uuid} and always clean up with DELETE /api/pages/custom/{page_id}.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - all review_request items verified green. The navigation.py /public refactor works as designed: merge navigation_items + custom_pages (show_in_nav+published+public), reserved slugs (blog/videos/news/about) route to /<slug>, others route to /page/<slug>, path-based dedupe prevents duplicates, defaults appended last.
